hypothesis: If we steer a frozen molecular LLM by adding CBM-learned, approximately orthogonal property vectors to its intermediate activations during generation, then it will more accurately match specified single and multi-property targets (lower MAE/Best100MAE) while preserving validity, novelty, and synthesizability and outperform vanilla, ActAdd, and fine-tuned baselines across in- and out-of-distribution settings because these vectors disentangle and linearly control property-relevant directions in the model’s representation space.
